dimensions.notes: cleanup is not an LLM owner. New `driver` pseudo-owner; nextNode intercepts owner===driver before spawn and returns action:cleanup.
Worktree removal is irreversible git. Explicit approval only: --approve or approval_gate=approved. not_required (small-reversible waiver) is insufficient.

Target = leaking per-run worktrees (.ditto/worktrees/*), the only thing DITTO creates and never reclaims. commit=user job, branch=none created, temp=arbitrary → excluded. Non-forced removal: dirty worktrees skipped, never destroyed.

questions.question: How is a deterministic cleanup node dispatched if it has no LLM owner?
questions.why_matters: It must not go through the spawn/delegation-packet path meant for LLM owners.
questions.answer: driver pseudo-owner; nextNode intercepts and emits action:cleanup; `autopilot cleanup` runs it.

questions.question: What authorizes the irreversible git step?
questions.why_matters: The contract mandates an explicit approval gate for irreversible git, not the auto-waiver.
questions.answer: Explicit only: --approve flag or approval_gate=approved; not_required does not authorize.

questions.question: What exactly does v0 cleanup remove?
questions.why_matters: Scope is irreversible; must be the non-arbitrary, real accumulating artifact.
questions.answer: Per-run worktrees only; non-forced (dirty skipped). commit/branch/temp out of scope.

assumptions.statement: driver pseudo-owner; nextNode intercepts and emits action:cleanup; `autopilot cleanup` runs it.
assumptions.label: hypothesis

assumptions.statement: Explicit only: --approve flag or approval_gate=approved; not_required does not authorize.
assumptions.label: hypothesis
